title: manager-prompt-placeholder-leak
description:
This is a continuation of manager-prompt-placeholder-leak. A factory-internal prompt placeholder leak recurred again in the current window, with the flagged watcher note reporting high-severity placeholder markers in a manager_watcher prompt near the start of the interval. The manager_watcher run itself completed successfully at 2026-05-29T18:52:10.840072+00:00, so the issue is not presenting as a runtime failure; it is a prompt-assembly defect visible in prompt logs.

The pattern is now sustained across many consecutive summarizer intervals, with no evidence of resolution in the prior concern history. Impact in this window is limited because the affected manager persona still returned successfully and other factory signals were quiet, but the defect remains a recurring plumbing issue in internal prompt construction rather than a story-specific error.

A separate failed dev run for story 27 ended with "tests not green after run" after 245.542 seconds and $0.65059 of spend, but the error text is ambiguous and there is no retry pattern in this window. That event does not change the main concern. Long-standing orphaned worktrees were also reported as unchanged, which indicates continuity rather than a new signal.

Given the persistence and high diagnostic clarity of the placeholder leak, this remains an L3-worthy concern. The most plausible area is prompt assembly or dispatch plumbing for the manager_watcher path, but the evidence here is limited to recurrence rather than a newly observed failure mode.

evidence.summary_excerpt: The previously escalated prompt placeholder leak recurred again in a manager_watcher prompt at the start of the window.

evidence.excerpt: {"noteworthy":"High-severity placeholder markers were again found in a manager_watcher prompt, continuing the previously escalated prompt-assembly leak."}
{"noteworthy":"One failed dev run for story 27 with \"tests not green after run\"; this is an ambiguous test-red outcome and not a retry pattern in this window."}

{"noteworthy":"Long-standing orphaned worktrees persist: story 33 remains in tests_red, and stories 51-57 and 59-64 have worktrees with missing DB rows; the set is effectively unchanged from the prior note."}

escalation_reason: Repeated high-severity prompt placeholder leakage in manager_watcher prompts has persisted across many consecutive watcher-note intervals, including this window, with no sign of resolution. L3 judgment is needed because this is a clear factory-internal prompt/dispatch plumbing pattern rather than a story-domain issue, and recurrence means the defect is still present in active manager runs.
